behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary (three collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary step), not inside one pure function. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required to prove this behavior; in-process source fakes can expose missing/overwritten sections. Rejected shortcut: not choosing Integration just because feature is multi-module; choosing it because this behavior specifically is cross-module data wiring. Rejected higher levels: behavior text only requires 'content passed' to summary step, not real external API behavior or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector-orchestration and payload-building modules together, with deterministic fakes for each source returning distinct sentinel values. Assert the exact payload sent to summary generation contains all three source sections and values without loss or collision.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience across module interactions: source failure handling, section-level fallback annotation, and continued report assembly. A fake source raising an error can reproduce the failure mode; real network/OS is not required. Rejected shortcut: not selecting lowest just because mocks exist; Unit is too low because the behavior depends on orchestration across source adapter + report builder. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about fallback note and continuation, not real infrastructure semantics or LLM judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration with two healthy source fakes and one failing fake (timeout/exception). Assert run completes, output report includes a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section, and other sections are still present.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on summary content quality ('accurately represents key values'), which is a model judgment/output property. Stubbed summaries would always pass formatting checks and hide hallucination/omission failures. Rejected shortcut: deterministic assertion does not justify Integration; this behavior evaluates what the model actually says. Rejected Workflow: behavior text covers one summary-generation decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model using controlled inputs with known key values; score output for factual inclusion/consistency (e.g., required value mentions, no contradictions) over repeated runs and fail on accuracy below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is end-to-end infrastructure: external source calls plus actual email delivery/inbox receipt. In-process mocks can hide SMTP/provider/inbox routing failures. Rejected shortcut: not defaulting to Integration for multi-module feature; this behavior explicitly requires real delivery outcome ('reaches ... inbox'). Rejected Agentic: behavior text is delivery success, not evaluation of model-output quality; any model step can be treated as non-quality-critical for this assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled job in an environment using reachable test endpoints and a real test mailbox. Verify one run produces an email received by configured recipients with expected report structure and source sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is orchestration/persistence logic: detect send failure, persist artifact, then consume persisted artifact on next run for retry. This can be reproduced with an email adapter fake that raises unreachable errors plus real local filesystem in-process. Rejected shortcut: not choosing lowest because mock exists; Unit is too low since behavior spans delivery adapter, persistence, and scheduler/run-state handoff. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require validating real SMTP/network semantics, only unreachable-path handling and next-run retry behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real persistence path (temp dir) with mail adapter fake that fails first run and succeeds second run. Execute two runs of scheduler entrypoint. Assert run1 writes fallback file, run2 retries and sends previously generated report, and persisted retry state is cleared/updated appropriately.
